title: The Ultimate Benediction I HG Chanchalapati Dasa I SB 5.3.13 I 18.06.2021
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 5, Chapter 3, Text No. 13
By His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami
Dear Lord, here is the great King Nabhi
whose ultimate goal in life is to have a son like You.
Your Lordship, his position is like that of a person
and begging for a little grain.
Maharaj Nabhi is so desirous of having a son
that he is worshipping You for a son
although You can offer him any exalted position
including elevation to the heavenly planets
The priests were a little ashamed
that King Nabhi was performing a great sin
and a sacrifice just to ask the Lord's benediction for a son.
The Lord could offer him promotion to the heavenly planets
Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has taught us
how to approach the Supreme Lord
and ask him for the ultimate benediction.
He did not want to ask the Supreme Lord for anything material.
He did not want to ask the Supreme Lord for anything material.
But an intelligent devotee doesn't ask the Supreme Lord
He wants to be engaged perpetually in the loving service of the Lord.
He does not want promotion to the heavenly planets or mukti, liberation from material bondage.
If this were the case, Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu would not have said,
Mama Janmani Janmani, it doesn't matter to a devotee whether or not he takes birth life after life,
as long as he remains a devotee.
Actually, eternal liberty means returning home back to Godhead.
A devotee is never concerned about anything material.
Although Nabhi Maharaj wanted a son like Vishnu, wanting a son like God is also a form of sense gratification.
A pure devotee wants only to engage in the Lord's loving service.
